subject: refactor(theme): align with THEME_DARK_MODE reference — catalog module, applyTheme helper, meta generator, allowlist
body:
After the full DaisyUI migration landed on this branch, a side-by-side
comparison with docs/implementations/THEME_DARK_MODE.md surfaced seven
divergences from the reference pattern. None were bugs that would
immediately manifest, but all were the kind of structural drift that
decays into bugs the moment someone adds a theme or changes a default.
This pass closes the gap on every actionable divergence.

Divergences addressed:

1. No theme catalog module — theme names and meta colors were hardcoded
   module constants at the top of AppContext.jsx.
2. No applyTheme() helper — the same dual-layer DOM mutation (.dark +
   data-theme + meta theme-color + Chart.js defaults) was inlined in
   three places (AppContext darkMode effect, App.jsx embed override,
   inline flash prevention script). Any drift between them = visual bugs.
3. PWA meta theme-color hex values hardcoded — reference wants a build
   script that reads DaisyUI's theme/*/object.js and converts oklch to
   hex at build time so the values never drift.
4. No theme ID allowlist in flash prevention script — a removed theme
   ID in localStorage would produce an unstyled first paint.
5. Cross-tab storage listener only handled darkMode — no forward-compat
   for lightTheme/darkTheme keys a theme picker would write.
6. No explicit aria-label on the theme toggle menu item — the visible
   label "Light mode"/"Dark mode" describes a destination, not an action.
7. No getStoredTheme/persistTheme/validLightTheme/validDarkTheme helpers
   exposed as part of a catalog module.

New files:

scripts/generate-theme-meta.mjs — imports each registered DaisyUI theme's
  object.js via dynamic import(), runs an inlined oklch->hex converter
  (oklch -> oklab -> linear sRGB via LMS -> gamma-corrected sRGB -> hex,
  ~30 lines, no color library dependency), and writes
  dashboard/js/generated/themeMeta.js with META_COLORS, IS_DARK, and
  THEME_NAMES exports. Validated against known values: lofi base-100
  oklch(100% 0 0) -> #ffffff, black base-100 oklch(0% 0 0) -> #000000.

dashboard/js/themes.js — theme catalog module. Exports LIGHT_THEMES,
  DARK_THEMES, DEFAULT_LIGHT_THEME, DEFAULT_DARK_THEME, validLightTheme,
  validDarkTheme, getStoredTheme, persistTheme, getMetaColor, and most
  importantly applyTheme(dark, themeName, skipPersist) — the single
  source of truth for .dark class + data-theme attribute + meta
  theme-color content + Chart.js defaults. Every theme-affecting caller
  now routes through this function. Imports META_COLORS/IS_DARK/
  THEME_NAMES from ./generated/themeMeta.js and Chart from chart.js
  (ES modules cache the Chart singleton shared with main.jsx and
  AppContext.jsx).

dashboard/js/generated/themeMeta.js — auto-generated, committed so fresh
  clones work without running the generator first. Header says DO NOT
  EDIT BY HAND. Regenerated by the prebuild hook on every npm run dev
  and npm run build.

Refactored:

dashboard/js/AppContext.jsx — removed LIGHT_THEME/DARK_THEME/
  LIGHT_META_COLOR/DARK_META_COLOR module constants and the direct
  `import { Chart as ChartJS } from 'chart.js'` (both moved to
  themes.js). darkMode useEffect collapsed from 30 lines of inlined DOM
  mutations to a single applyTheme(state.darkMode,
  getStoredTheme(state.darkMode)) call. Cross-tab storage listener now
  handles three keys — darkMode dispatches to the reducer, lightTheme
  and darkTheme call applyTheme(..., skipPersist=true) directly when
  the key matches the current mode.

dashboard/js/App.jsx — imports applyTheme + DEFAULT_*_THEME from
  themes.js. Embed override ?theme=light|dark calls applyTheme with
  skipPersist=true instead of doing its own classList + setAttribute
  dance. ?bg=... still sets --color-base-100 directly since it's a
  pure surface override, not a theme swap.

dashboard/index.html — inline flash prevention script gained
  LIGHT_THEMES/DARK_THEMES allowlists, a validTheme() helper, and a
  META map inline. Reads darkTheme/lightTheme from localStorage in
  addition to darkMode, validates each, falls back to defaults on
  miss. Applies .dark class + data-theme attribute + meta theme-color
  content before first paint. Script comment documents the four-place
  sync requirement (this script + themes.js + styles.css @plugin
  config + generate-theme-meta.mjs REGISTERED_THEMES).

dashboard/js/components/Header.jsx — theme toggle menu item gained an
  explicit ariaLabel: state.darkMode ? 'Switch to light mode' :
  'Switch to dark mode'. Visible label stays the same ("Light mode" /
  "Dark mode"). Comment explains why the destination-style label needs
  an action-style aria-label.

dashboard/js/components/HamburgerMenu.jsx — accepts an optional
  item.ariaLabel prop on menu items, applied as the button's aria-label
  attribute. Falls back to item.label for menu items that don't need
  a distinct screen-reader label.

package.json — dev, build, and build:lib scripts all prefix with
  `node scripts/generate-theme-meta.mjs &&` so the generated file is
  always fresh. Added `npm run generate-theme-meta` for manual
  invocation after adding themes.

Not addressed (feature decisions, not bugs):

- Approach A vs Approach B storage schema. This project has one theme
  per mode and no picker UI, so neither approach is "chosen" — we ship
  Approach A's key schema (darkMode + lightTheme + darkTheme) as a
  superset with the extra keys being forward-compat plumbing only.
  Documented in themes.js top comment.
- Theme picker UI in burger menu — tracked as optional follow-up in
  docs/TODO.md. Infrastructure (catalog, cross-tab sync, allowlist,
  persistTheme) is all in place.
- DaisyUI component classes instead of custom CSS — already tracked
  as optional follow-up. Separate incremental pass from theming.

Build: passes. CSS bundle 150.64 KB (unchanged — no CSS changes this
pass, only JS + HTML refactoring). 84 modules transform (up from 82
because themes.js and generated/themeMeta.js are new). Only CSS warning
is DaisyUI's internal @property --radialprogress (not ours).

Smoke test via vite preview + curl verified:
- HTML <html lang="en" class="dark" data-theme="black">
- Both <meta name="theme-color"> tags with media queries
- Inline flash prevention script contains LIGHT_THEMES, DARK_THEMES,
  'lofi': '#ffffff', 'black': '#000000', validTheme
- Built CSS contains both [data-theme="lofi"] and [data-theme="black"]
- All 14 critical custom class families present (.heatmap-0..4,
  .heatmap-cell, .filter-multi-select*, .settings-pane*, .detail-pane*,
  .error-boundary-card, .dashboard-header, .card, .btn-primary,
  .toast-success)

Grep verification: themes.js is the only file that touches
classList.add/remove('dark'), setAttribute('data-theme', ...), or
ChartJS.defaults. Zero duplication.

Tags: theme, daisyui, refactor, a11y, pwa, build
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-migration
Semver: minor
